_comment: Scenario 5: Geofence Breach — polygon violation triggers RTL

scenario.name: geofence_breach
scenario.description: Configure a tight geofence polygon. Waypoints intentionally placed near the boundary. The simulated pose will drift outside, triggering geofence violation and automatic RTL.

fault_sequence._comment: No explicit fault injection. The 4th waypoint (16,5) is outside the geofence polygon (max x=12). The drone exits the eastern boundary on the WP3(11,11)→WP4(16,5) leg, triggering FAULT_GEOFENCE_BREACH and RTL. WP4 is placed at y=5 (not y=15) to avoid the magenta cylinder at (14,13) — the original (15,15) diagonal passed within 0.1m of its surface.

manual_controls.notes: Place waypoints outside the geofence polygon to trigger breach. Adjust geofence.polygon vertices to change boundary. WP4 at (16,5) exits the eastern boundary (x=12) cleanly, avoiding the magenta cylinder at (14,13). altitude_floor_m=-5.0 prevents spurious floor violations during normal landing (drone at z=0 would otherwise trigger the 0m floor check).
